solution: 
```java
class Solution {
    public boolean reorderedPowerOf2(int n) {
        String digits = getDigitCount(n);
        for (int i = 0; i < 32; i++) {
            String powerDigits = getDigitCount(1 << i);
            if (digits.equals(powerDigits)) {
                return true;
            }
        }
        return false;
    }

    private String getDigitCount(int n) {
        StringBuilder sb = new StringBuilder();
        for (int i = 0; i < 10; i++) {
            sb.append(countDigit(n, i));
        }
        return sb.toString();
    }

    private int countDigit(int n, int digit) {
        int count = 0;
        while (n > 0) {
            if (n % 10 == digit) {
                count++;
            }
            n /= 10;
        }
        return count;
    }
}
```
efficiency: HIGH
time complexity: O(log n)
space complexity: O(1)
energy implications: LOW
explanation: The solution counts the digits of the input number and compares them with the digit counts of all powers of two up to 2^31. This ensures that we only need to check a fixed number of powers of two, making the solution efficient. The digit counting is done in constant space and logarithmic time relative to the input size.
